title: Would you bother to be friends with the Eva Trio
display_title: Would you bother to be friends with the Eva Trio
wikitext:
[]: Would you bother to be friends with the Eva Trio?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Thu Dec 22, 2011 4:17 am After watching and reading the EoE Screenplay, a particular deleted scene caught my eye: Shinji as an elementary school student, alone, crying at his desk. In the background, music and announcements indicating the end of the school day. Voice of a male classmate: Tch, he's obsessed with himself, just because he lives at the teacher's place! Voice of a female teacher: What a depressing child. Voice of a female classmate: Such a nuisance! Voice of a female classmate: He can't do anything by himself. Asuka as an elementary school student, sitting alone at her desk. In the background, the voices of children screaming as they play. (In German?) Voice of a male classmate: Her tests are the most important [thing], so she can't say anything [to us]. Voice of Asuka as an elementary school student: I just don't talk away stupidly! Voice of a male classmate: Pfft, that stuck-up, who do you think you are!? Voice of Asuka as an elementary school student: What? I'm not the same as you! Asuka's voice: Don't think that I'm the same!

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Thu Dec 22, 2011 4:35 am I think this might be for the chit-chat sub forum. I would be friends with both Shinji and Rei. But in middle school I would never have been friends with Asuka, mainly because of the social hierarchy. High school was a different story altogether. Fun fact Everybody in high school thought I was a mute till my sophomore year.

[]:             Quote Post by Maya Ibuki » Thu Dec 22, 2011 5:52 am I'm still in Middle School! (High school down here) Sure I'd bother to be friends with the Eva trio. Rei seems like a lonely person, and she seems full of potential, but she's just not showing it. She needs friends. Asuka may be stuck up, and too popular, but it's fine, because she's a good example of someone looking perfect on the outside, but flawed within. Even though Asuka does seem like a bitch, at times she is really sympathetic and deep. And Shinji.. yeah, simply because he is a boy.

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Thu Dec 22, 2011 6:07 am I don't think I would be friends with any of them. I'd be too shy to talk to them like anyone else in real life. Now, if by chance I had to talk to them, then yes, I suppose I would end up becoming friends with them. I'd expect Shinji and I would have a lot in common.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 22, 2011 6:23 am It's quite funny actually. During late middle school and early high school, I would say that I was pretty much a Shinji, and my two major love interests during that period were respectively an Asuka and a Rei. I admit that the Asuka was probably a hormonal thing, but the Rei really left an impact and I was very drawn to her mysterious, antisocial nature. In that respect, I would probably wish to be great friends with Rei, though probably not Asuka. I'd probably never want to be friends with Shinji. However, if I were to take myself as I am now and apply it to the topic, then I'm not quite sure how I would answer. I'm much more interested in down-to-earth people, and none of that is really apparent in any of the three.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Dec 22, 2011 8:45 am Well, that is certainly an interesting question. Under the premise that I'm 14 years old again and they are just "normal" kids and not The Children , I would probably like the usual myself keep aloof from them. Shinji I might get along with but he wouldn't strike me as particular interesting. It could change for whatever reason, though I think we would most likely just remain simple classmates without ever realising how alike we actually are. Rei is even more distant and it would be very hard for me to form bonds with her considering how reserved I am myself, which is a pity, because once you have worked yourself through her shell she is a very sweet girl. Asuka is kind of an ambivalent case. I dislike her type of attention seeking, however I cannot deny that the 14 year old me would have secretly the hots for her. I'd like to image, since I speak German, maybe we could develop a connection but occasionally I can be denser than Shinji when it comes to reading "female signals", so any deeper approach might end as sudden as it may have begun. Now Toji and Kensuke on the other hand, I'm sure we would become total bros, as they are easy to work with and they share the same hobbies as me, mainly sports and nerdy stuff that is.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Dec 22, 2011 12:02 pm As above, under the premise that I'm my fourteen-year-old self: Asuka yeah. The only kind of people I actually know are her kind of people, whether male or female. Inevitably ends badly and is fun while it lasts, in a masochistic sense. And just keeps going on and on, because both parties are too desperate for some kind of friendship to actually go break it off. I don't know, I have pretty fond memories of some of those people, and I think some of them were assholes. I might regret approaching her (would definitely regret, let's face it) but I think I would do it. Although my fourteen-year-old self was a bit of a pansy, so maybe I'd just let myself down. Shinji would be one of those guys I'd hang out with out of boredom/pity and eventually our friendship would disintegrate due to lack of interest. I saw one of those guys while I was Christmas-shopping with my sister the other day, awkward as fuck and unlike Shinji he wasn't even attractive. Coming home for Christmas sucks. This time my fourteen-year-old self's aggressive need to feel superior to someone would help me out, though. I would probably stick around him just to prove to myself that I wasn't the king of misery. Rei no. She probably smells like blood anyway, I doubt I'd ever even consider going near her.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Dec 22, 2011 2:12 pm I tend to try to befriend almost everyone, so I'd at least talk to all of them a few times. I was almost identical to Shinji when I was 14, so we'd likely be friends. Rei I'd approach immediately, because I've always liked shy girls, and her hospital-like scent would be enjoyable. Asuka would be an interesting case. I've a friend who is very similar to her, but is also a chronic liar. The two of us tend to be best friends for about six months, hate each other for two weeks, then be friends again. Asuka and I would probably share a similar relationship. If she did start to try to attract me, I wouldn't notice at first, but someone else would point it out in time for me to tell her I return my feelings. Touji I wouldn't get along with, due to lack of any common interests. Kaworu would probably be my friend, if only because he'd hang around Shinji and Rei. I'm very quick to fall in love, so I'd wind up feeling filio, eros, and agapao for Shinji, Rei, and Asuka simultaneously.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 22, 2011 3:02 pm Let's go back, way back... I'm at school, I'm 14, and is the big sensation for the album buying members of class. I'm at an all-boys school, so that rules two of the cast out straight away -- unless we increase the improbability factor by assuming that either of them wanted to take Classical Greek (there was one girl who joined the school for just that reason) and then we get more contrafactual than expected, by having to hypothesize how I would interact with any girl at that age. Having a summer birthday, and showing little inclination to athletic prowess, Shinji would be one of the younger members of the class, so probably would end up as one of the "rabbits" in games lessons. That would mean joining the loosely aggregated anti-clique that was basis for much of my social life at the time, so by default, yeah, he'd be one of the guys I counted as a fellow sufferer. It would depend on his other interests -- is he a train spotter or more of a sci-fi geek? -- how much further it went.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Dec 22, 2011 4:47 pm Yeah, If I was a middle school student again I'd probably end up hanging out with Shinji (friend-of-a-friend kind of deal most likely, as I hung out with plenty of Kensukes and a few Tojis) and would definately be crushing awkwardly in Rei's general direction. Asuka would be a problem as we'd be in the small gifted classes together and I wouldn't be able to stand her bullshit, so I'd probably be actively making her life hell and vice-versa.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Thu Dec 22, 2011 6:47 pm I don't really feel like it would be possible to just assume there was a normal, "non Lilith" Rei that would have the same personality when it seems like her personality revolves around her bizarre existence. I guess this is a thread of suspending disbelief but that particular point bothered me for some reason. But anyway: I think most males would say they would be friends with Asuka and Rei, but in reality probably wouldn't be able to. I imagine I'd find Asuka to be too abrasive and Rei too cold to make any sort of meaningful friendship or relationship with either of them, especially assuming you don't know all about their backstories and whatnot. Shinji would be a more likely friend for someone like me, but then again it's hard to bring yourself back to being 14 and say for sure. 14 year olds can be pretty stupid after all. Interesting topic nonetheless. We often feel sympathy for the characters when watching the show, and yet the truth is we probably wouldn't treat them much differently than anyone else did either. I guess it highlights the real tragedy of the situation.

[]:             Quote Post by Cosman246 » Thu Dec 22, 2011 8:28 pm Hmm. Given the way I was at that age, I'd probably try to be friends with Shinji and Rei (Asuka would probably be associated with people who I didn't want to be associated with; i.e. bullies)--but given my luck and relationships, I'd probably end up alone--alienated from Shinji through lack of many common interests and general coldness, and teased for trying to talk to Rei. It'd be hard for me to talk to them, and if they ended up like the rest of my school, well, I'd be lonely again. There's a reason Gendo is my favorite character

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 22, 2011 8:47 pm I pretty much WAS Shinji in middle school (and high school) so yeah, I'd befriend him no problem. The Asuka type of people intimidated the hell out of me while the Rei type of people I (more or less) got along with.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Dec 22, 2011 8:52 pm What? Are you serious? Shinji's avoidant in the extreme, Asuka's projected personality is grade A bitch, and Ayanami just doesn't care . How could I be friends with any of them, even if I wanted it? It's not really a question of whether or not I'd be willing; I'd be anyone's friend, even then. It was more a question of whether or not it would even be possible given who and what they are. I don't think it would be, personally. That's kind of the point of the exercise.

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Thu Dec 22, 2011 9:34 pm Given the fact that I have never been good at making friends coupled with their personalities it would most likely never happen for me.....

[]:             Quote Post by Xacebans » Thu Dec 22, 2011 10:35 pm Depends on which one. Perhaps my pity-side would take over in terms of Shinji since he'd probably be the kid who sits alone, who my group of amazing but rabid girl friends would flock to and comfort him in our nerdy ways. We are known for doing this already. Knowing him, he would try to push us away though. Rei? Probably the same situation. Asuka would probably already have charmed the athletic girls (and therefore the popular ones). They would deal with how irritating she can be (if they can deal with themselves they can deal with her). Basically, that's what would happen, at least in my school environment or one similar.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Thu Dec 22, 2011 11:57 pm Azathoth wrote: Rei no. She probably smells like blood anyway, I doubt I'd ever even consider going near her.

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Fri Dec 23, 2011 12:17 am Aiko Heiwa wrote: I don't think I would be friends with any of them. I'd be too shy to talk to them like anyone else in real life. Now, if by chance I had to talk to them, then yes, I suppose I would end up becoming friends with them. I'd expect Shinji and I would have a lot in common.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri Dec 23, 2011 12:48 am Friends with the pilots? An interesting question. I think Shinji would be the easiest to befriend. We'd probably already be traveling in some of the same circles so they're be plenty of chances for a friendship to form. I doubt I'd be friends with Asuka. We'd be in two different worlds most of the time so I'd have little chance to make friends. At most she'd just be someone I'd say high to sometimes, but definitely not a friend. rhfxz1s wrote: I don't really feel like it would be possible to just assume there was a normal, "non Lilith" Rei that would have the same personality when it seems like her personality revolves around her bizarre existence. I guess this is a thread of suspending disbelief but that particular point bothered me for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Dec 23, 2011 1:26 am It's easy for me to look back and say I'd be Shinji's friend. I have a way too strong conscience, so I'd probably try to interact with him (because I felt bad for him). Around people I know I tend to relax my guard a lot, so it'd be a lot easier with friends near. Honestly, though, it probably depends on my class. If I'm a class with no one I'm overly familiar with I'm more likely to act like Shinji myself, quiet and reserved. There's still a chance I'd try, but it's less likely. Asuka and Rei are easy answers. I was (and still am to an extent) way too scared of chicks IRL to try and be friends with them. Small talk was fine, but friendship? Hell, I think the only chick I started small talk with was the one next to my locker, and that only took me a few months....
